 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9","title":"[EPIC] Ship readiness: docs, CI checks, release workflow, versioning","description":"# [EPIC] Ship readiness (docs + CI + release)

## Mission
Make v0.1.0 shippable:
- clear docs
- reliable CI signals
- predictable version/build metadata

## Why
A tool that controls terminals needs high trust and clear operator UX.

## Definition of done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test` pass in CI.
- README + quickstart lets another engineer install and run wa.
- Version/build metadata is visible (`wa --version`).

## Success Criteria
- CI runs fmt/clippy/tests/e2e reliably and preserves artifacts on failure.
- Release workflow/versioning produces reproducible binaries with build metadata.
- Docs/README/quickstart are sufficient for a new contributor to install and run wa.

## Testing
- CI verification:
  - CI must run the full quality gate (fmt/clippy/tests) and E2E, and upload artifacts on failure.

- Release smoke tests:
  - After packaging, run `wa --version` and `wa --help` and a minimal `wa doctor --format json` scenario to ensure the binary is functional.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:33:23.215105369Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:07:22.496766524Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-ci","area-docs","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.9","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.1","title":"CI pipeline: fmt/clippy/test/bench (feature matrix for mcp/web/tui/sync/browser)","description":"# Task: CI pipeline

## Goal
Ensure core quality checks run automatically on changes and failures are diagnosable from logs/artifacts.

## Core checks (blocking)
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`

## Feature matrix smoke (blocking or warn-only; decide explicitly)
Build/test the crate under common feature combinations so optional integrations don’t silently rot.

Baseline matrix (PLAN Appendix A/F driven):
- default features
- `--features mcp`
- `--features web`
- `--features tui`
- `--features sync`
- `--features browser`

Vendored lane (PLAN Appendix H):
- When `--features vendored` exists, CI must at least:
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - and run a minimal smoke/unit subset that exercises vendored wiring

This is intentionally separated from the ROI decision (`wa-nu4.4.1.6`): once vendored is enabled, CI must keep it from rotting.

## Docs correctness lane
- Add a docs-smoke step/job that keeps README quickstart commands executable (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

## Related CI jobs (separate tasks)
- E2E job for real WezTerm scenarios: `wa-nu4.3.9.6`
- Setup idempotency job (safe dotfile patching in temp home): `wa-nu4.3.9.8`
- Vendored maintenance lane: `wa-nu4.4.1.7`

## Artifacts and logging
- On failure, upload:
  - test logs (stdout/stderr)
  - E2E artifacts (runner logs + captured outputs)
  - benchmark outputs when applicable

## Testing
- CI “tests the tests”:
  - Add a minimal job that prints the exact rustc/cargo versions and the exact feature-matrix commands being executed.
  - Ensure CI captures and uploads logs/artifacts for failing jobs so failures are actionable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures are actionable (clear logs, no flaky tests).
- Optional features (especially vendored) do not silently break over time.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.10","title":"Beads hygiene: eliminate bd lint template warnings (Acceptance Criteria headings, missing sections)","description":"# Task: Beads hygiene (template lint to zero)

## Goal
Make the Beads plan itself reliable and self-documenting by ensuring bd lint produces zero template warnings.

This improves:
- onboarding for future contributors
- clarity during implementation
- confidence that tasks have explicit acceptance criteria

## Scope
- For all open tasks/features/bugs:
  - ensure the description includes the required section heading(s):
    - tasks/features: ## Acceptance Criteria
    - bugs: ## Steps to Reproduce, ## Acceptance Criteria
  - ensure the acceptance criteria are concrete and testable

## Change discipline
- Do not delete or truncate existing content.
- Prefer minimal edits:
  - rename "## Acceptance" -> "## Acceptance Criteria"
  - add a missing section at the end if absent

## Acceptance Criteria
- bd lint --status open produces no warnings.
- New issues created in this project follow the template by default.


## Testing
- Run `bd lint` locally after any template edits.
- Add a simple CI check (if desired) that runs `bd lint --status open` and fails on warnings.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.2","title":"Docs: README/quickstart + architecture + CLI/robot/MCP reference","description":"# Task: Documentation for v0.1

## Goal
Make the project approachable for a new contributor and operable for users.

This bead is intentionally self-contained and mirrors PLAN Appendix A + F so contributors don’t need to dig through planning docs.

## Deliverables
### 1) README (operator-first)
- What wa is (WezTerm mux “terminal hypervisor” for agent fleets).
- Quickstart:
  - install wa
  - `wa setup`
  - `wa watch`
  - `wa status`
  - `wa query` / `wa events`
- Safety guarantees:
  - observe vs act split
  - no silent gaps
  - policy-gated sending + approvals

### 2) Architecture doc
- Ingest → Storage (SQLite+FTS5) → Pattern engine → Workflow engine → Robot/MCP.
- Deterministic state via OSC 133.
- Explicit GAP semantics.

### 3) CLI reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Human-oriented commands (rich output by default via `rich_rust`):
- `wa status` — panes + inferred agent state
- `wa watch` — run daemon (foreground/background)
- `wa stop` — stop watcher in workspace safely
- `wa events` — recent/unhandled events
- `wa query` — FTS search
- `wa send` — guarded send to a pane
- `wa reserve` — reserve a pane for exclusive use
- `wa reservations` — list active reservations
- `wa approve` — grant allow-once approvals
- `wa workflow` — run a workflow manually (guarded)
- `wa audit` — audit trail of actions (redacted)
- `wa history` — action timeline + undoability
- `wa undo` — undo supported actions (with confirmation)
- `wa rules` — list/test rule packs, show matching traces
- `wa accounts` — list/refresh accounts, show rotation picks
- `wa setup` — canonical WezTerm configuration (local/remote)
- `wa doctor` — environment checks (wezterm presence, DB health)
- `wa diag bundle` — sanitized diagnostic bundle export
- `wa export` — export slices/history/audit
- `wa web` — optional HTTP server (`fastapi_rust`) if enabled
- `wa tui` — optional TUI (`charmed_rust`) if enabled
- `wa sync` — optional sync (`asupersync`) if enabled

Robot mode commands (stable JSON envelope; token-efficient):
- `wa robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail N] [--escapes]`
- `wa robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs N]`
- `wa robot search "<fts query>" [--pane-id <id>] [--since <iso8601>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot events [--unhandled] [--pane-id <id>] [--type <event>] [--limit N]`
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations`
- `wa robot accounts [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot accounts refresh [--service <openai|anthropic|google>]`
- `wa robot rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa robot rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- `wa robot quick-start`

### 4) MCP reference (PLAN Appendix A)
Tools (names are short and obvious):
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- `wa.reserve`
- `wa.release`
- `wa.reservations`

Resources:
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- `wa://reservations`

### 5) Library integration map (PLAN Appendix F)
| Library | Role in `wa` |
|---------|--------------|
| `cass` (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) | Correlation + session archaeology; used in status + workflows |
| `caut` (`/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker`) | Usage truth + selection; used in accounts + `handle_usage_limits` |
| `rich_rust` | Human-first CLI output (tables/panels/highlight) |
| `charmed_rust` | Optional interactive TUI (pane picker, event feed, transcript viewer) |
| `fastmcp_rust` | MCP tool surface for agent control (mirrors robot mode) |
| `fastapi_rust` | Optional HTTP server for dashboards/webhooks (read-only first) |
| `asupersync` | Remote bootstrap/sync layer (configs, binaries, DB snapshots) |
| `playwright` | Automate device auth flows with persistent profiles |
| `ast-grep` | Structure-aware codebase scans in “unstick agent” workflows |

## Testing / doc correctness
Docs must not drift.

- The quickstart commands shown in README should be validated regularly:
  - either by referencing existing E2E scripts, or
  - by adding a small “docs smoke” CI step that runs the exact commands from the docs in a controlled environment (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

- Robot mode docs should match the schema contract:
  - reference the versioned JSON schemas and keep field names consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new engineer can get to a running watcher and see events with no oral tradition.
- CLI/robot/MCP references match implemented command surfaces and schemas.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.3","title":"Version/build metadata: `wa --version` shows semver + build info + enabled features","description":"# Task: Version/build metadata (`wa --version`)

## Goal
Make it easy to know exactly what build of wa is running when debugging bugs, comparing machines, or filing issues.

This becomes non-optional once we span multiple machines and (optionally) a vendored WezTerm build.

## Output contract
- `wa --version` prints a **single, stable** line by default (human-friendly, scriptable).
- `wa --version --verbose` prints a multi-line block with detailed metadata.
- Output must not require a TTY.

## Fields to include (when available)
- `wa` semver
- build timestamp (UTC)
- git commit hash (if built from a git checkout)
- rustc version
- target triple
- enabled cargo features (mcp/web/tui/sync/browser/vendored/etc.)

Vendored mode (PLAN Appendix H):
- when `--features vendored` is enabled, include:
  - vendored WezTerm commit hash (the pinned commit)
  - any compatibility/version string we can reliably extract

## Why this matters
When automation spans multiple machines, “what version is running?” is the first diagnostic question. Vendored mode adds an additional critical dimension (which WezTerm commit?).

## Testing
- Unit tests for `--version` output:
  - non-empty
  - contains semver
  - stable (no ANSI, no random ordering)

- Verbose output tests:
  - includes the detailed fields and feature list
  - when built with vendored feature (in CI matrix), includes vendored commit hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa --version` output is stable and does not require a TTY.
- `wa --version --verbose` provides enough metadata to uniquely identify the build.
- Vendored builds surface vendored commit metadata deterministically.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.4","title":"Release packaging: reproducible builds + single-binary install path","description":"# Task: Release packaging

## Goal
Provide a simple, reliable way for users to install and update wa:
- build a single lean binary
- ship artifacts with enough metadata to debug
- keep installation friction low

## Deliverables
- Release build produces a single binary (`cargo build --release`).
- Provide at least one installation path:
  - downloadable release artifact (preferred)
  - optional `cargo install --path .` for devs

- Document:
  - minimum supported OSes
  - required external deps (e.g., WezTerm installed)
  - feature flags and what they enable

## Reproducibility & safety
- Aim for reproducible builds (same inputs → same hash) where feasible.
- Publish checksums for release artifacts.
- Never require users to run unsafe install commands.

## Testing
- Smoke tests for the released binary:
  - `wa --version --verbose`
  - `wa --help`
  - `wa doctor` (in a minimal environment)

- Feature-matrix build verification in CI (tracked in CI bead):
  - default features
  - `--features mcp`
  - `--features web`
  - `--features tui`
  - `--features sync`
  - `--features browser`
  - when vendored exists: `--features vendored`

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can install and run `wa --help` without cloning the repo (via a release artifact).
- Release artifacts include build metadata (`wa --version --verbose` is meaningful).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.5","title":"Dogfooding + fixture capture: real WezTerm runs for compaction/usage/auth (all agents)","description":"# Task: Dogfooding + fixture capture

## Goal
Validate wa in real conditions and capture stable fixtures for regression tests.

## Scenarios to run
- Codex:
  - compaction detection + handle_compaction
  - usage limit path (if reachable) or simulated fixture
  - device auth flow (openai)
- Claude Code:
  - compaction detection + handle_compaction
  - usage limit banner detection
  - cass correlation sanity
- Gemini:
  - session summary parsing
  - auth flow presence

## Outputs
- Add captured transcripts to the corpus (sanitized).
- Record any new banners/prompts that require new patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one end-to-end successful run of:
  - capture → detect → workflow → verify
in a real WezTerm pane.


## Testing
- Sanitization requirements:
  - Before committing any captured transcript into fixtures, scan for secrets and replace with placeholders.

- Regression integration:
  - Each captured transcript must be wired into the corpus runner with an expected detection JSON so drift becomes a failing test.
